questions.question: 1 (a) The student uses a thermometer to measure room temperature θ R . Room temperature is 22 °C. parts.answer:  on answer line correctly calculated to 2 or more significant figures unit: g / cm answer given to 2 or 3 significant figures 1 May/June 2025 Question Answer Marks , °C 1 suitable scales (occupying at least ½ the grid) 1 plots correct to  ½ small square AND precise plots good best-fit line judgement, thin, continuous line 1 • some form of insulation • add a lid • shield from draughts • white / shiny surface May/June 2025 Question Answer Marks incident ray in correct quadrant at 40°  2°  = 48°  2° angle of reflection drawn 50°  2° justification to include idea of results being equal within the limits of experimental error OR unequal if beyond the limits of experimental error • thin pins • thin lines / sharp pencil • view bases of pins / pins vertical • pins far apart / at least 5 cm May/June 2025 Question Answer Marks 4 MP1 circuit used: correct circuit diagram MP2 measurements made: measure wire diameter AND measure voltage and current OR record voltmeter and ammeter readings MP3 analysis of readings: calculate / work out resistance of wire MP4 performing the investigation: repeat for at least two other diameters of wire MP5 variable kept constant: length / material of wire MP6 table: with columns for diameter of wire, voltage, current and resistance each column must be labelled with both quantity and unit MP7 conclusion: plot a graph of resistance against diameter OR an answer which suggests comparing values of diameter AND resistance in table
